preview_text:
The model rates Turkey a modest favourite, assigning them a 38% win probability against USA's 31%. That 7-percentage-point gap suggests a competitive encounter rather than a clear dominance scenario. The underdog USA nonetheless retain genuine chances across all three outcomes.

The forecast shape points toward a tightly contested match. The draw probability sits at 31%, indicating that a stalemate remains highly plausible. The spread between the top two outcomes—Turkey's 38% and the draw at 31%—is narrow enough that the result remains genuinely uncertain heading into the fixture. No single outcome commands overwhelming backing.

The sportsbook consensus diverges noticeably from the model. Books favour USA's chances at 38%, a 7-percentage-point uplift from the model's 31%, while assigning the draw only 27% probability compared to the model's 31%. Turkey's consensus price of 35% sits 3 points below the model. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the divergence warrants attention from users evaluating which framework better reflects match conditions.
